alerts.title: Iran launches ballistic missiles and drones at Kuwait, Bahrain, and regional targets
alerts.summary: Iran launched multiple ballistic missiles and drone strikes targeting Kuwait airport (lat 32.13, lon 53.21), Bahrain (lat 32.16, lon 53.2), and allegedly UAE facilities on June 2-3. CENTCOM confirmed US forces intercepted missiles headed for Kuwait and Bahrain; however, Kuwait's Defense Ministry officially confirmed airport strike damage. Rybar Telegram (~110K views) reports escalation continuing nightly in Persian Gulf with no de-escalation signals.

alerts.title: Kuwait airport sustains direct Iranian drone and missile strike damage
alerts.summary: Multiple credible sources (BBC, Guardian, Al-Monitor, Reuters, Washington Post) report Iranian drone and missile attack on Kuwait International Airport (lat 32.13, lon 53.21), with confirmed infrastructure damage. Kuwait Defense Ministry officially acknowledged strike. Incident marks direct attack on US-allied civilian infrastructure and signals Iran expanding target set beyond military assets.

alerts.title: US retaliates with strikes on Iranian oil tanker and Qeshm Island radar sites
alerts.summary: Reuters and multiple outlets report US fired Hellfire missile at Iranian blockade-busting tanker (lat 31.94, lon 53.26) near Strait of Hormuz. CENTCOM confirmed self-defense strikes on Qeshm Island (lat 32.3, lon 52.95) radar and communications infrastructure in response to Iranian attacks. IRGC claimed counterstrikes on US Fifth Fleet HQ and airbase, escalating tit-for-tat exchange.
alerts.region: Strait of Hormuz / Qeshm Island / Persian Gulf

alerts.title: Trump administration rhetoric escalates on Iran nuclear deal; ceasefire collapse imminent
alerts.summary: US sentiment analysis shows 'trump' (58 hits) and 'iran' (12 hits) among top trending terms amid primaries and tariffs discussion. Time Magazine reports Trump stating it is time 'one way or another' for Iran to make deal while insisting talks ongoing—contradicting reports of suspended negotiations. Sentiment remains near parity (45% positive, 46% negative), but Trump's hardline language signals administration willingness to pursue military escalation without diplomatic off-ramp.

alerts.title: Three-carrier task force sustains forward deployment; USS Gerald R. Ford joins Mediterranean
alerts.summary: Assets feed confirms USS Gerald R. Ford (Carrier) newly deployed to Mediterranean (lat 34.36, lon 29.71) alongside continued presence of USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea, lat 20.53, lon 60.24) and USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Red Sea, lat 15.41, lon 41.91). Three-carrier global posture with supporting destroyers (Carney, Mason, Laboon) and amphibious assault ship USS Bataan maintains maximum forward presence across three theaters during critical escalation window.
alerts.region: Arabian Sea / Red Sea / Mediterranean / Persian Gulf

analysis_note: US-Iran military escalation has entered sustained tit-for-tat phase with no diplomatic off-ramp; Iran at 60% enrichment with 1-2 week breakout timeline; threat level remains critical (9/10); three US carriers forward-deployed; ceasefire collapsed following Israeli operations in Lebanon.
